text: The buyer has the stronger interpretation. The remedy covenant says all remedies at law or in equity remain available for uncured service failures. The service-credit schedule is limited to routine outages, so the plain meaning preserves damages for a severe uncured outage.

reasoning_signature.rule_trigger: ordinary meaning of the remedy covenant text
reasoning_signature.exception_or_defense: service-credit limitation does not displace clear remedy covenant
reasoning_signature.reasoning_path: plain meaning of unambiguous covenant preserves damages for severe uncured failures

text: A text-first reading favors the buyer because the all-remedies clause is broader than the operational service-credit schedule. The schedule can be read as applying to routine outages only, leaving damages available for severe uncured failures.

reasoning_signature.rule_trigger: unambiguous contract text and ordinary meaning
reasoning_signature.exception_or_defense: routine outage credit provision is limited
reasoning_signature.reasoning_path: plain meaning controls; broad remedy covenant survives narrower service-credit language

text: If the remedy covenant and service-credit schedule cannot be reconciled, the ambiguity should be construed against Apex because Apex drafted the agreement. That fallback canon supports the buyer's damages interpretation.

reasoning_signature.reasoning_path: competing remedy clauses create ambiguity; construe against Apex as drafter

text: The seller has the stronger argument if the service-credit schedule is treated as the exclusive operational remedy for outages. On that reading, damages are displaced by the specific remedy schedule.

reasoning_signature.outcome: seller limits buyer to service credits
reasoning_signature.exception_or_defense: specific service-credit schedule overrides broad remedy language
reasoning_signature.reasoning_path: specific limitation prevails over general all-remedies clause
